epic_name: MVP PRD — Mobile Quarterly Export in 10 Minutes (UK Sole Traders, Bank-Feed First)
summary: Decomposed the MVP into granular backend, export, evidence, voice, UI, and verification tasks that align to the recommended build order and acceptance tests while keeping each task independently allocatable.
warnings: The epic does not specify the mobile stack, backend framework, database, or Open Banking provider; tasks assume those choices can be made within implementation without changing MVP scope.
Authentication, onboarding, and secure token handling are inferred as required foundation work even though they are not broken out explicitly in the epic.
UI-visible tasks include local start-script, localhost access, smoke-start, and screenshot evidence requirements per the UI delivery viewability contract.
Receipt OCR/extraction is treated as best-effort metadata extraction, not a hard accuracy gate, because the epic defines confirmation-first matching.
Quarter packaging is assumed to export locally or to app-managed storage with traceable links; final storage destination is not specified in the epic.

tasks.title: Define MVP domain schemas and category taxonomy
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish the canonical data contracts, audit fields, and import foundations needed by all downstream MVP flows.
tasks.purpose: Create the minimum viable domain model, export field definitions, blocker-related transaction attributes, and stable category set so every later service uses the same contracts.
tasks.input: Epic data model, blocking rules, category list, export field requirements, and acceptance tests.
tasks.output: Versioned schema definitions for User, BusinessProfile, BankAccount, BankTransaction, TransactionClassification, Evidence, EvidenceLink, Quarter, QuarterMetrics, Rule, and export row contracts.
tasks.action: Author the canonical schemas, enums, validation rules, and relationship notes required for ingestion, triage, evidence matching, quarter readiness, and export generation.
verification: Schema covers every MVP entity and every field named in the Quarterly Pack outputs.
Blocking-rule attributes are explicitly represented and nullable rules are documented.
Audit fields exist for all user-editable classification data.
Category codes match the epic list exactly and separate categories from tags.

tasks.title: Implement secure sign-up and sole-trader onboarding flow
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish the canonical data contracts, audit fields, and import foundations needed by all downstream MVP flows.
tasks.purpose: Enable first-time setup for the MVP user type so the app can create an account, persist a sole-trader business profile, and gate unsupported business types.
tasks.input: A1 schemas and the epic first-time setup journey.
tasks.output: Onboarding service and client flow that creates a user and BusinessProfile configured for sole trader only.
tasks.action: Build sign-up and onboarding logic, restrict business selection to sole trader for MVP, and persist required profile and disclaimer state for later quarter/export use.
verification: A new user can complete sign-up and store a sole-trader business profile.
Unsupported business types are not selectable or are clearly blocked in MVP.
Disclaimer acknowledgement is persisted before bank connection proceeds.
Onboarding output provides the identifiers needed by bank connection and inbox setup.

tasks.title: Integrate Open Banking connection and token lifecycle
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish the canonical data contracts, audit fields, and import foundations needed by all downstream MVP flows.
tasks.purpose: Allow the user to connect a read-only bank feed using a provider-managed flow with secure credential and token handling.
tasks.input: A1 schemas, A2 onboarding flow, and epic in-scope Open Banking requirements.
tasks.output: Bank connection service, persisted BankAccount records, and secure token management for read-only transaction access.
tasks.action: Implement provider connection flow, store account metadata, secure refresh/access token handling, and a stable connection status model for later imports.
verification: A connected account can be created and stored without exposing raw secrets in logs or client state.
Token refresh and reconnect paths are handled for expired or revoked connections.
Only read-only banking permissions are requested for the MVP flow.
Connected account metadata is sufficient to start initial and incremental transaction syncs.

tasks.title: Build idempotent transaction import and 90-day backfill
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish the canonical data contracts, audit fields, and import foundations needed by all downstream MVP flows.
tasks.purpose: Import the latest approximately 90 days of bank transactions during setup and support refreshes without creating duplicates.
tasks.input: A1 schemas, A3 bank connection capability, and epic non-functional requirement for idempotent imports.
tasks.output: Transaction ingestion pipeline with initial backfill, incremental refresh, duplicate prevention, and normalized BankTransaction records.
tasks.action: Implement transaction fetch, normalization, deduplication keys, import checkpoints, and refresh behavior for connected bank accounts.
verification: Initial import can ingest roughly 90 days of transactions for a newly connected account.
Re-running the same import does not create duplicate BankTransaction records.
Normalized transactions include all fields needed for classification, quarter metrics, and export.
Import failures are logged with retry-safe state rather than leaving partial duplicate data.

tasks.title: Implement transaction classification model with full audit trail
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn imported transactions into resolved or blocking items using fast micro-decisions, rules, and complete edit traceability.
tasks.purpose: Persist classification state separately from raw bank transactions so category, business/personal, splits, confidence, and edit history are traceable.
tasks.input: A1 schemas and imported transactions from A4.
tasks.output: TransactionClassification persistence layer with change history and default state initialization for newly imported transactions.
tasks.action: Create classification records, attach audit entries for user edits, and initialize confidence/default values for imported transactions.
verification: Every imported transaction can be associated with a classification record.
User edits write previous and new values with actor and timestamp.
Split and business/personal states support nullable unresolved values for blocker evaluation.
Classification data is queryable without mutating the original bank transaction payload.

tasks.title: Build inbox micro-decision actions and suggestion application
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn imported transactions into resolved or blocking items using fast micro-decisions, rules, and complete edit traceability.
tasks.purpose: Support the MVP triage actions that resolve transactions quickly through taps or later voice intents.
tasks.input: B1 classification model, category taxonomy, and epic inbox action requirements.
tasks.output: Application services or APIs for category assignment, business/personal tagging, split percentage updates, and duplicate resolution state changes.
tasks.action: Implement the command handlers for all core micro-decisions, including suggestion acceptance, manual override, split updates, and duplicate dismiss or merge resolution.
verification: Each supported micro-decision updates classification state and audit history correctly.
A transaction can be moved from unresolved to resolved when the required fields are supplied.
Duplicate resolution supports both dismiss and merge outcomes without data loss.
Manual overrides are distinguishable from automated suggestions for later PDF audit counts.

tasks.title: Implement blocker evaluation and transaction resolution rules
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn imported transactions into resolved or blocking items using fast micro-decisions, rules, and complete edit traceability.
tasks.purpose: Encode the exact blocking-export logic so readiness metrics and Finish Now queues are deterministic and traceable.
tasks.input: A1 schemas, B1 classification state, B2 action outcomes, and epic blocking rules.
tasks.output: Rule service that marks transactions as resolved, blocking, or non-blocking and exposes blocker reason codes.
tasks.action: Build the rules engine that evaluates transaction state against the MVP export blockers while keeping missing evidence explicitly non-blocking.
verification: Transactions are marked RESOLVED only when all required fields are present per epic rules.
Evidence or receipt absence never creates a blocking state.
Reason codes are emitted so queues can be ordered and UI can explain why an item blocks export.
Rule outputs are deterministic for the same transaction state.

tasks.title: Implement simple merchant rules for recurring categorisation defaults
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn imported transactions into resolved or blocking items using fast micro-decisions, rules, and complete edit traceability.
tasks.purpose: Reduce future triage volume by applying user-defined merchant patterns to later transactions while preserving override capability.
tasks.input: A1 Rule entity, B1 classification model, B2 action handling, and epic merchant rule scope.
tasks.output: Rule creation, storage, and application service that maps merchant patterns to category and optional default business or split values.
tasks.action: Implement rule creation from user actions, merchant matching on import or refresh, and safe application of suggested defaults to applicable transactions.
verification: A rule can be saved from a user classification decision and applied to future matching merchants.
Automatic rule application remains auditable and user-overridable.
False-positive rule application can be corrected without corrupting historical audit data.
Rule application measurably reduces unresolved item counts in repeated import scenarios.

tasks.title: Build quarter model, readiness metrics, and blocking queue ordering
tasks.workstream_goal: Convert classified transaction data into a quarter-close workflow and accountant-friendly export pack with immediate export enablement once blockers reach zero.
tasks.purpose: Represent quarter periods, compute readiness percentages, and produce the ordered Finish Now queue that drives the 10-minute close workflow.
tasks.input: A4 imported transactions, B3 blocker evaluation, and epic quarter readiness formulas and ordering requirements.
tasks.output: Quarter and QuarterMetrics services that compute totals, blocking count, readiness percentage, and ordered blocking queue for a selected quarter.
tasks.action: Implement quarter period selection, transaction aggregation, readiness calculations, and queue ordering by uncategorised, personal missing, split missing, then duplicates.
verification: A quarter with 200 transactions and 8 blockers yields a blocking count of 8 and the correct readiness percentage.
Blocking queue ordering matches the epic order exactly.
Export eligibility flips to enabled immediately when blocking count reaches zero.
Quarter metrics update after each micro-decision without requiring a full re-import.

tasks.title: Generate Transactions.csv and EvidenceIndex.csv exports
tasks.workstream_goal: Convert classified transaction data into a quarter-close workflow and accountant-friendly export pack with immediate export enablement once blockers reach zero.
tasks.purpose: Produce the detailed accountant-facing CSV exports that carry quarter transaction traceability and evidence linkage.
tasks.input: A1 export contracts, B1 classifications, C1 quarter metrics, and D3 confirmed evidence links when available.
tasks.output: Transactions.csv and EvidenceIndex.csv generation service that emits valid files for the selected quarter, including empty EvidenceIndex.csv when needed.
tasks.action: Build CSV serializers, field mapping, null-handling, ordering, and export packaging for transaction and evidence index outputs.
verification: Transactions.csv contains every field required by the epic in a stable, documented order.
EvidenceIndex.csv is exported even when no evidence exists for the period.
Matched evidence identifiers and confirmed bank transaction links are preserved accurately.
CSV totals and record counts reconcile with quarter transaction selection.

tasks.title: Generate QuarterlySummary.csv and QuarterlyPack.pdf
tasks.workstream_goal: Convert classified transaction data into a quarter-close workflow and accountant-friendly export pack with immediate export enablement once blockers reach zero.
tasks.purpose: Create the summary outputs required for quarter handoff, including category totals, unresolved counts, audit counts, readiness, and evidence coverage.
tasks.input: C1 quarter metrics, B1 classifications, C2 detailed exports, and epic PDF content requirements.
tasks.output: QuarterlySummary.csv and QuarterlyPack.pdf with period totals, category highlights, readiness state, audit counts, and evidence coverage metrics.
tasks.action: Implement summary aggregation and PDF rendering logic for the required 1-2 page accountant-friendly pack.
verification: QuarterlySummary.csv totals reconcile with detailed transaction export data.
QuarterlyPack.pdf shows period, totals, readiness, category highlights, audit counts, and evidence coverage.
PDF readiness is 100% at export time and export is blocked otherwise.
Generated artifacts are readable and suitable for handoff without proprietary tool dependencies.

tasks.title: Assemble final quarterly pack and export delivery flow
tasks.workstream_goal: Convert classified transaction data into a quarter-close workflow and accountant-friendly export pack with immediate export enablement once blockers reach zero.
tasks.purpose: Package all four export artifacts into a single user-triggered flow with traceable metadata, consistent naming, and completion logging.
tasks.input: C2 CSV exports, C3 summary and PDF outputs, and quarter export gating from C1.
tasks.output: Export orchestration flow that builds the Quarterly Pack, stores or shares artifacts, and records export completion metadata.
tasks.action: Implement the end-to-end export command that validates readiness, generates all artifacts, packages them coherently, and records the export event for analytics.
verification: Export produces all four required artifacts in one completion flow.
The export command refuses to run while blocking transactions remain.
Artifact names, timestamps, and traceability metadata are consistent across a single pack.
Export completion is logged for funnel and completion-rate metrics.

tasks.title: Implement evidence capture storage and metadata extraction pipeline
tasks.workstream_goal: Allow optional receipt evidence to be captured, extracted, and matched to bank transactions without ever becoming an export blocker.
tasks.purpose: Store receipt or invoice evidence, extract best-effort metadata, and persist extraction confidence for later matching suggestions.
tasks.input: A1 Evidence schema and epic evidence capture requirements.
tasks.output: Evidence ingestion pipeline with file storage, evidence records, extracted amount/date/merchant metadata, and confidence scoring.
tasks.action: Build evidence upload or capture intake, persist evidence files and metadata, and run best-effort extraction for downstream candidate matching.
verification: Evidence can be stored and retrieved with a durable storage link and metadata record.
Extraction output captures amount, date, merchant, and confidence when available.
Evidence ingestion failures do not block transaction export flows.
Evidence records are available for later matching suggestions even when extraction is partial.

tasks.title: Build confirm-first evidence matching candidate service
tasks.workstream_goal: Allow optional receipt evidence to be captured, extracted, and matched to bank transactions without ever becoming an export blocker.
tasks.purpose: Rank top bank transaction candidates for a piece of evidence using the matching logic described in the epic while preserving user confirmation as the final step.
tasks.input: A4 bank transactions, D1 extracted evidence metadata, and epic matching criteria.
tasks.output: Matching service that returns top 3 candidate bank transactions with reason chips and confidence metadata.
tasks.action: Implement candidate scoring using amount, date proximity, and fuzzy merchant matching, then return the three best matches with explanations suitable for UI presentation.
verification: The service returns at most three ranked candidates for a given evidence item.
Returned candidates include merchant, date, amount, and human-readable match reasons.
No automatic link is created without explicit user confirmation.
No-match scenarios are supported without generating blockers or errors.

tasks.title: Persist evidence links and unmatched states with user confirmation
tasks.workstream_goal: Allow optional receipt evidence to be captured, extracted, and matched to bank transactions without ever becoming an export blocker.
tasks.purpose: Record confirmed evidence-to-transaction links, confirmation metadata, and explicit no-match or later decisions for audit-friendly exports.
tasks.input: D2 candidate service, A1 EvidenceLink schema, and epic confirmation requirements.
tasks.output: EvidenceLink persistence and workflow handlers for confirm, no match, and later outcomes.
tasks.action: Implement the commands that confirm a candidate, defer matching, or mark no match while updating evidence-related queue items and export linkage data.
verification: A confirmed match creates an EvidenceLink with user confirmation metadata.
Choosing No match or Later preserves the evidence record without blocking quarter export.
Confirmed links remove the related evidence task from any active queue.
EvidenceIndex.csv can reflect confirmed and unmatched evidence states correctly.

tasks.title: Deliver mobile inbox exception queue screen
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide the mobile-first screens and voice-assisted workflows that make quarterly triage and close achievable in minutes.
tasks.purpose: Give the user a fast inbox view that surfaces only actionable transaction items and supports tap-based micro-decisions with low latency.
tasks.input: A4 imported transactions, B2 micro-decision actions, B3 blocker statuses, and MVP ongoing triage journey.
tasks.output: Mobile inbox UI with transaction list, suggested actions, category/business/split controls, and queue filtering for flagged items.
tasks.action: Implement the inbox screen, hook it to classification actions and blocker state, and optimize it for quick one-handed triage on a typical mobile device.
verification: Provide a start script that launches the app locally and prints the localhost URL for the inbox workflow.
Document the exact localhost URL to open for inbox verification.
Run a startup smoke test confirming the UI boots without immediate dependency crashes.
Capture at least one screenshot of the working inbox screen in the epic verification folder.
Inbox shows flagged/actionable items and allows category, business/personal, split, and duplicate actions.
Typical inbox load path stays under the stated 2-second target on representative local test data.

tasks.title: Deliver quarter readiness and Finish Now screen
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide the mobile-first screens and voice-assisted workflows that make quarterly triage and close achievable in minutes.
tasks.purpose: Expose the core quarter-close experience with readiness percentage, remaining item count, ordered blocking queue, and export gating.
tasks.input: C1 quarter metrics, C4 export flow, and epic quarter screen requirements.
tasks.output: Quarter screen UI showing readiness headline, item count, Finish Now queue, and export action state.
tasks.action: Implement the quarter screen and Finish Now queue, wiring live metric updates so export state changes immediately when blockers are cleared.
verification: Provide a start script that launches the app locally and prints the localhost URL for the quarter screen.
Document the exact localhost URL to open for quarter-close verification.
Run a startup smoke test confirming the quarter UI loads without immediate crashes.
Capture screenshots of the quarter screen and Finish Now queue in the epic verification folder.
The screen renders the headline format like '92% ready — 8 items left' using live data.
Export is visibly disabled when blockers exist and becomes enabled immediately at zero blockers.

tasks.title: Deliver evidence capture and match confirmation UI flow
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide the mobile-first screens and voice-assisted workflows that make quarterly triage and close achievable in minutes.
tasks.purpose: Allow users to attach receipts, review the top three candidate bank matches, and confirm or defer the link through a simple bottom-sheet flow.
tasks.input: D1 evidence ingestion, D2 candidate matching, D3 confirmation handlers, and epic evidence UX requirements.
tasks.output: Mobile evidence flow with receipt capture entrypoint, candidate bottom sheet, match reason chips, and No match or Later actions.
tasks.action: Implement the evidence attach workflow and confirmation-first candidate selection UI for inbox or quarter-close contexts.
verification: Provide a start script that launches the app locally and prints the localhost URL for the evidence workflow.
Document the exact localhost URL to open for evidence matching verification.
Run a startup smoke test confirming the evidence UI launches without immediate crashes.
Capture screenshots of the candidate match bottom sheet in a working state in the epic verification folder.
Each candidate shows merchant, date, amount, and match-reason chips with the first candidate visually prominent.
User can confirm a match or choose No match or Later without creating export blockers.

tasks.title: Implement voice intents, confirmation chip, and undo flow
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide the mobile-first screens and voice-assisted workflows that make quarterly triage and close achievable in minutes.
tasks.purpose: Support the MVP voice command set for inbox and Finish Now workflows while keeping every applied action immediately visible and easily reversible.
tasks.input: B2 micro-decision actions, D3 evidence confirmation actions, and epic voice command set and confirmation-chip rule.
tasks.output: Voice intent handling layer and UI feedback components for category, business/personal, split, attach receipt, match ranking, and no-match commands.
tasks.action: Implement voice intent parsing or provider integration, map intents to the same action handlers as taps, and show a single confirmation chip with one-tap undo.
verification: Voice commands 'Category: {X}', 'Business', 'Personal', 'Split {n}%', 'Attach receipt', 'Match first/second/third', and 'No match' are recognized and routed correctly.
Applied voice actions show a single confirmation chip summarizing the change and offering undo.
Provide a start script that launches the app locally and prints the localhost URL for voice-flow verification.
Document the exact localhost URL to open for testing voice-enabled screens.
Run a startup smoke test confirming voice-enabled UI boots without immediate crashes.
Capture screenshot evidence of the confirmation chip in the epic verification folder.
The acceptance test 'Voice Category: Travel applies and is undoable with one tap' passes.

tasks.title: Instrument MVP analytics and quarter-close funnel metrics
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove the MVP meets its speed, completion, confidence, and compliance expectations through measurable telemetry and robust validation.
tasks.purpose: Capture the product metrics defined in the epic so the team can measure time-to-export, completion rate, blocker counts, and voice success.
tasks.input: User flows from onboarding, inbox, quarter close, evidence matching, voice actions, and export completion.
tasks.output: Analytics event taxonomy, metric aggregation logic, and dashboards or reports for MVP success metrics.
tasks.action: Instrument key events and derive the median quarter-close duration, completion rate, average blockers after 30 days, and voice success rate for MVP reporting.
verification: Events exist to calculate each epic metric without ambiguous joins or missing timestamps.
Time from opening the quarter screen to export generation can be computed accurately.
Voice success can distinguish successful application without edit from failed or corrected attempts.
Analytics instrumentation does not log secrets or raw bank credentials.

tasks.title: Harden security performance and legal disclaimers for MVP release
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove the MVP meets its speed, completion, confidence, and compliance expectations through measurable telemetry and robust validation.
tasks.purpose: Meet the explicit non-functional requirements for secure storage, encryption, idempotency, inbox performance, and user disclaimer visibility before release.
tasks.input: A3 bank integration, A4 import pipeline, E1 and E2 UI flows, and epic non-functional requirements.
tasks.output: Validated security and performance checklist with implemented safeguards, performance measurements, and visible disclaimer coverage.
tasks.action: Review and implement the NFR safeguards, benchmark inbox load, verify encryption and token handling paths, and place the required non-tax-advice disclaimer in relevant user flows.
verification: Credentials and tokens are stored securely and are not exposed in logs or client caches.
Encryption in transit and at rest is documented or enforced for the chosen stack.
Inbox load is measured and meets the sub-2-second typical-device target on representative data.
The app displays a clear disclaimer that it is not tax advice and the user remains responsible.
Idempotent import behavior remains intact after security and performance hardening.

tasks.title: Build end-to-end MVP acceptance and regression suite
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove the MVP meets its speed, completion, confidence, and compliance expectations through measurable telemetry and robust validation.
tasks.purpose: Lock the epic acceptance tests into automated or repeatable verification so the MVP can be reviewed and regression-tested with confidence.
tasks.input: Completed ingestion, classification, quarter, export, evidence, and voice workflows across prior tasks.
tasks.output: End-to-end test scenarios and fixtures covering quarter-close, export, evidence confirmation, and voice undo acceptance criteria.
tasks.action: Create deterministic test data and regression scenarios that exercise the full MVP from imported transactions through export generation and evidence or voice workflows.
verification: A scenario with 200 transactions and 8 blockers shows the correct items-left count before resolution.
Resolving the 8 blockers enables export immediately and generates all four artifacts.
Evidence attachment requires user confirmation of the match or explicit No match handling.
Voice 'Category: Travel' applies successfully and is undoable with one tap.
Regression coverage includes duplicate handling, split percentages, and empty-evidence export cases.
